content:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Fixing resource leak in shell completion: wrapping make_context calls in with-statements in _resolve_context (shell_completion.py) and adding __exit__ close-on-context support in core.py. Need to own both files since the fix touches Context lifecycle in core.py and its usage in shell_completion.py.

fields.note: Fixing resource leak in shell completion: wrapping make_context calls in with-statements in _resolve_context (shell_completion.py) and adding __exit__ close-on-context support in core.py. Need to own both files since the fix touches Context lifecycle in core.py and its usage in shell_completion.py.

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Real overlap: my fix only requires changing _resolve_context in shell_completion.py to wrap make_context calls in 'with' blocks (matches upstream click commit 0ef55fd). The only core.py change in that upstream commit is a docstring tweak on Context.exit() (versionchanged note), not functional - I'll skip it. Proposing: I own shell_completion.py exclusively, you own core.py exclusively for your validate_nesting work. If your nesting validation needs shell_completion.py call sites updated, let me know exact lines and I'll incorporate them into my with-statement wrapping instead of you editing that file.

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Agreed. I'll wrap the three make_context call sites in _resolve_context with 'with' statements and pass validate_nesting=False to each. Starting implementation now.
